questions.question: A circle has a radius of 5. What is the circumference?
A circle has center (2, 3) and passes through the point (5, 3). Write the equation of the circle.
questions.explanation: Radius is the distance from (2, 3) to (5, 3): r = 5 − 2 = 3. Standard form is (x − h)² + (y − k)² = r².

questions.question: A sector of a circle with radius 6 has a central angle of 60°. What is the arc length?
A line is tangent to a circle with center O and point of tangency T. The radius OT has length 4. If a point P on the tangent line is 3 units from T, what is the distance OP?
questions.explanation: A tangent is perpendicular to the radius at the point of tangency. Triangle OTP is right-angled at T, so OP = √(4² + 3²) = √25 = 5.

questions.question: A sector of a circle with radius 8 has a central angle of 90°. What is the area of the sector?
The equation of a circle is x² + y² = 25. What are the coordinates of the center and the radius?
questions.explanation: The equation x² + y² = r² has center at the origin. Here r² = 25, so r = 5.
